sections.paragraph:
for אֲמֵנֶת f. with suff. אֲמִתִּי, אֲמִתֹּו.

(1) firmness, stability, perpetuity. Isa 39:8, שָׁלֹום וֶאֱמֶת “peace and stability,” i.e. firm and stable peace, by ἓν διὰ δυοῖν, compare No. 2; also security. Jos 2:12, אֹות אֱמֶת “a token of security,” i.e. secure or certain.

(2) faithfulness, fidelity, in which any one is consistent and performs promises (Treue und Glauben). Applied to a people, Isa 59:14, 15 Isa 59:15to a king, Psal. 45:5 to God, Psa 30:10 71:22 91:4 . Very often joined with חֶסֶר Psa 25:10 40:11 57:4 , 11 Psa 57:11 108:5 138:2 and by ἓν διὰ δυοῖν the constant and perpetual favour of God is to be understood. עָשָׂה חֶסֶד וֶאֱמֶת עִם “to shew sincere good will to any one,” Gen 24:49 47:29 Jos 2:14 2Sa 2:6 15:20 .

(3) probity, uprightness, integrity of mind. Exo 18:21, אַנְשֵׁי אֱמֶת שׂנְאֵי בֶצַע “upright men, hating covetousness.” Neh 7:2 Jud. 9:16 , 19 Jdg 9:19. Opp. to רֶשַׁע Pro 8:7. Specially it is integrity (of a judge), justice. Psa 19:10, “the judgments of God are upright, just;” Isa 16:5 Pro 29:14 also sincerity opp. to dissimulation, Jos 24:14 1Sa 12:24 1Ki 2:4 Isa 10:20.

(4) truth, opp. to falsehood, Gen 42:16 Deu 22:20 2Sa 7:28 אֲמָרִים אֱמֶת “words (which are) truth,” Pro 22:21. Applied to the word of God, Psa 119:142 to prophecies, Jer 26:15 to a servant of God, Isa 42:3. Hence אֱמֶת יְהֹוָה “the truth of the Lord,” often used for his true doctrine and worship, Psa 25:5 26:3 86:11 .
